title: MoA Proposer Output
description: Schema returned by a proposer agent (codex, gemini, or sonnet) at Layer 1 of the mixture-of-agents pipeline. All three proposers produce this same shape so the orchestrator can stitch them together for the refiner and aggregator layers.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the proposer agent that produced this output. Lowercase, dash-separated, 1-32 chars. Built-in defaults: codex (gpt-5.4), gemini (gemini-2.5-pro), sonnet (claude-sonnet-4-6). User-named providers (e.g. cursor-grok) follow the same pattern.

summary.description: One to two paragraph plain-language overview of the proposed approach. No markdown headings.

plan.description: Ordered list of steps the implementer should take. Each step must be concrete enough to act on.

step.description: Imperative action the implementer should take. Start with a verb.

why.description: Reasoning for this step. Why is it necessary, what does it unlock?

files_touched.description: Repo-relative file paths this step would create, modify, or read. Use path:line format when a specific line is relevant.

evidence.description: Claims this step depends on, each backed by either a code citation or an external source. Refiners will verify these.

type.description: code = citation from the local repo. external = citation from a web source.

file.description: Repo-relative path. Required when type=code; set to null when type=external.

line.description: Line number in the file. Required when type=code; set to null when type=external.

url.description: URL of the external source. Required when type=external; set to null when type=code.

snippet.description: Short verbatim quote from the source that supports the claim. Required for external; optional-but-null for code.

claim.description: What this evidence proves or supports. Always required.

risks.description: Things that could go wrong with this step, dependencies it has, or assumptions it makes.

open_questions.description: Questions the spec does not answer that the implementer will need resolved before proceeding.

alternatives_rejected.description: Approaches you considered and rejected. Naming what you ruled out is as valuable as naming what you chose.

research_sources.description: External sources you consulted while building this plan. Include at least 2 (aim for 5+). Mix of official docs, blog posts, GitHub repos, papers, Stack Overflow answers, anything authoritative.

summary.description: What this source says, in your own words.

relevance.description: Why this source matters to the plan you proposed.
